mappings.rationaleAI: Broadly addresses branches of private law including property, contracts, torts, and civil procedure (A-E).
Directly relevant to section B, examining marriage, divorce, children's legal status, and parental rights.
Laws protecting intangible property rights -- copyright, patents, and trademarks -- are the central subject (A.5.b, A.5.c, A.5.d).
Labour law governing employment relationships and workers' rights is the central focus (D.4).
Examines laws governing mergers, monopolies, and competitive practices in business (D.3.d, C.5.b).
Explores comparisons between common law and civil law approaches to property, contracts, and torts (A, C, D).
Traces the historical development of American private law including property, tort, copyright, and commercial law (A, C, D).
Examines civil procedural law including jurisdiction, venue, trials, appellate courts, and juries (E).
The jury in civil trials and the rendering of judgment in civil cases are central topics (E.2).
Arbitration as an alternative method of resolving commercial and civil disputes is examined (E).
Negotiation and settlement as alternatives to civil litigation are explored (E).
Tort liability in medical contexts -- including negligence, consent, and manufacturer's liability -- is the central subject (C.4).
Injuries to personality and invasions of privacy are examined as matters of tort law (C.5.a).
Private international law, including conflicts of laws and foreign judgments, is addressed (E.1.b).
Financial reporting and auditing are relevant to corporate law and business association governance (D.3.c).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationaleAI: The legal framework governing financial transactions, credit, and banking regulation is examined (D.2).
Quantitative aspects of corporate finance including risk management and arbitrage pricing inform securities law (D.3.c).
Economic theory underlying commercial law -- competition, monopoly, market structures, and antitrust -- provides essential context (D.2, D.3).
Tax law relevant to corporate finance and business association governance is addressed (D.3.c).
Laws concerning the marriage contract across jurisdictions and their legal effects are examined (B.1.a).
Rule of law principles underlie property rights, due process, and civil procedure (A, E).
